commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b958c8de3a7e563fe2e57c99b47ad9b193ceecd0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads `stuck_recovery` through embedded-run diagnostic recovery, classifies stuck recovery by active model/tool state, adds regression coverage, and repairs two focused test/tooling failures.

PR surface: Source +72, Tests +157, Other +2. Total +231 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: the issue logs show stuck recovery producing an external-abort failover path, and PR-head code shows the remaining reply-run fallback still drops `stuck_recovery`. I did not force the intermittent live opencode-go outage.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow mechanical repair remains: preserve `stuck_recovery` through the reply-run fallback path and add focused regression coverage.

target.title: fix(agent): classify stuck recovery as idle timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Fixes #93610. Diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborted active embedded runs with the same downstream semantics as user/restart/superseded aborts, so failover could see an external abort and surface `Request was aborted` instead of using the configured idle-timeout fallback path.
- Why it matters / User impact: Cron/provider sessions that stall while a model call is actively in flight can still use the existing idle-timeout rotate/fallback path, while stale post-model recoveries and side-effecting tool stalls are kept out of automatic replay paths.
- Solution: `stuck_recovery` is no longer classified from an "ever started a model call" flag. It now uses active/in-flight model-call state, and active potential-side-effect tool executions still take precedence as tool-execution timeouts.
- CI repaired: the current-head `checks-node-core-fast` mock leakage was fixed by making the two `agent-scope.js` test mocks partial mocks with `resolveSessionAgentIds`; the `checks-node-core-tooling` timeout path now waits for response-body reader cancellation before the client exits.
- What did not change: no provider catalog changes, no OpenAI-compatible stream parser changes, no opencode-go payload wrapper changes, no fallback policy rewrite, and no gateway protocol/config changes.
- Fixes #93610

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:**
```text
Diagnostic stuck recovery keeps the internal stuck_recovery abort reason and classifies retry safety from current active work, not from stale historical model-call state.
```

- **Behavior or issue addressed:**
```text
The issue trace showed diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborting an active embedded run, after which failover saw externalAbort=true and surfaced Request was aborted despite fallbackConfigured=true.

This patch keeps three replay-safety outcomes distinct:
- active model-call stall with no active potential-side-effect tool => model idle timeout, eligible for the existing rotate/fallback path;
- active side-effecting tool stall => tool-execution timeout, not idle timeout, so failover does not replay the tool call;
- stale post-model / pre-model / unknown stuck recovery => external-abort-like path, not a replay path.
```

- **Real environment tested:**
```text
Local OpenClaw existing-PR worktree on Linux.
Current HEAD: b958c8de3a7e563fe2e57c99b47ad9b193ceecd0
Runtime proof command imported production stuck-recovery classifier and embedded-run registry modules from the same current-head worktree with node --import tsx.
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```sh
node --import tsx <current-head stuck-recovery runtime proof>
OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=unit-fast-stuck-recovery-targets.json \
  pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts \
  --reporter=verbose \
  --testNamePattern "active tool execution counters|classifyStuckRecoveryAbort|stuck session recovery|embedded-agent runner run registry"
OPENCLAW_LOCALE=en OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=unit-fast-agent-scope-repro-patterns.json \
  pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=tooling-openai-chat-tools-only.json \
  pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts \
  --reporter=verbose
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
```

- **Evidence after fix:**
```json
{
  "head": "b958c8de3a7e563fe2e57c99b47ad9b193ceecd0",
  "classifications": {
    "modelCallStall": "model_idle_timeout",
    "sideEffectingToolStall": "tool_execution_timeout",
    "stalePostModelOrUnknownStall": "external_abort"
  },
  "abortAndDrain": {
    "aborted": true,
    "drained": true,
    "forceCleared": false
  },
  "observedAbortReasons": [
    "stuck_recovery"
  ]
}
```

```text
Focused stuck-recovery tests:
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  54 passed | 69 skipped (123)

Current-head unit-fast mock leakage regression:
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  83 passed (83)

Current-head tooling timeout regression:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  18 passed (18)

Type checks:
pnpm tsgo:core exited 0
pnpm tsgo:test:src exited 0

git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD exited 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:**
```text
The current-head runtime proof observes stuck_recovery being forwarded through abortAndDrainEmbeddedAgentRun, draining without force clear, and the classifier returning:
- active model-call stuck recovery => model_idle_timeout;
- active side-effecting tool stuck recovery => tool_execution_timeout;
- stale post-model / unknown stuck recovery => external_abort.

The targeted unit-fast shard reproduces the non-isolated agent-scope mock-leakage pattern that failed current-head CI and now passes. The tooling timeout test covers the response-body timeout path that failed current-head CI and now passes.
```

- **What was not tested:**
```text
Unavailable external live proof: I did not reproduce the intermittent live opencode-go cron outage, nor did I run a live opencode-go/deepseek-v4-flash provider stream against opencode.ai under the reporter's provider account/config.

Completed local/current-head verification: production-module stuck-recovery runtime proof, focused regression tests, unit-fast CI leakage regression, tooling timeout regression, core/test-src type checks, and diff check.

The provider-owned stream termination behavior is covered by separate provider-boundary PRs; this PR proves the downstream diagnostic recovery and failover-classification boundary.
```

- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix for the downstream diagnostic recovery / embedded-run failover classification boundary; complementary to provider-owned stream guard fixes.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** The previous `status: ⏳ waiting on author` blocker is addressed by replacing stale model-call history with active model-call state and by supplying current-head proof.
- **RF-002:** Added a regression showing a stale post-model recovery with no active model call and no active side-effecting tool stays `external_abort`.
- **RF-003:** Active potential-side-effect tools still classify as `tool_execution_timeout`, so stale active runs after a model call has ended do not become retry/fallback paths.
- **RF-004:** The provider-boundary PRs remain separate. This PR is scoped as downstream recovery hardening; maintainers can land it alongside or after a provider-owned stream guard.
- **RF-005:** The narrow mechanical repair is implemented: the attempt uses active model-call count and the missing stale post-model regression is present.
- **RF-006:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` now passes `modelCallActive: activeModelCallsForStuckRecovery > 0` into stuck-recovery classification.
- **RF-007:** Re-ran the focused stuck-recovery Vitest command on current HEAD; it passed with `Test Files 4 passed` and `Tests 54 passed | 69 skipped`.
- **RF-008:** `pnpm tsgo:core` passed on current HEAD.
- **RF-009:** `pnpm tsgo:test:src` passed on current HEAD.
- **RF-010:** `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD` passed on current HEAD.
- **RF-011:** Maintainer concern from vincentkoc is addressed directly: the code classifies the stuck phase first and only maps active model-call stalls to idle timeout; stalled side-effecting tools remain tool-execution timeouts to avoid replaying side effects.

## Origin / follow-up

N/A — independent downstream recovery root cause. The linked provider-boundary PRs are competition/scope context for #93610, not a merged-origin follow-up.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **#93965:** Provider-owned opencode-go stream guard. It addresses the upstream stream boundary so stalled opencode-go provider streams do not rely on the later stuck-session watchdog. This PR does not replace #93965.
- **#93640:** Earlier provider-scoped stalled stream guard. It overlaps the provider-boundary direction and remains separate from this downstream recovery classification boundary.
- **Related open PR scan:** The related open work is provider-boundary focused; this PR stays at the downstream embedded-run recovery/failover classification boundary and does not add or change provider wire contracts.
- **This PR:** Downstream embedded-run recovery safety. It fixes what happens after diagnostic stuck-session recovery aborts an active embedded run: the internal `stuck_recovery` reason must survive the registry boundary, and the active attempt must classify replay safety before failover decides whether retry/fallback is allowed.
- **Why this can land alongside a provider fix:** Even with a provider-owned guard, the generic stuck-session recovery path still needs replay-safe semantics. A model-call stall can use idle-timeout fallback; a side-effecting tool stall must not be replayed; stale/unknown recovery should remain external-abort-like.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/stuck-recovery-abort.test.ts`.
- **Target test files:** `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/stuck-recovery-abort.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts`, `src/flows/channel-setup.test.ts`, `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`, and `test/scripts/openai-chat-tools-client.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** Diagnostic stuck recovery carries `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`; model-call stalls are replay-safe idle timeouts only while a model call is active and no active potential-side-effect tool execution exists; side-effecting tool stalls are classified as tool-execution timeouts and do not trigger automatic fallback/retry; stale post-model stuck recovery remains external-abort-like.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The bug is at the abort-classification boundary between diagnostic recovery and embedded attempt failover, not in provider-specific SSE parsing. The tests cover the classifier, active model-call lifecycle, active tool side-effect signal, diagnostic recovery, registry abort/drain reason propagation, and the two current-head CI regressions without changing public provider behavior.

## Scope boundary

- **Scope boundary:** This PR only changes internal embedded-run recovery classification, diagnostic model-call lifecycle bookkeeping, the minimal test mocks that were leaking in non-isolated unit-fast CI, and one tooling-client timeout cleanup path that failed current-head CI. It does not change provider streaming, model routing, fallback selection policy, channel delivery, plugin contracts, public config, or persisted schema.
- **Large diff explanation:** The full PR diff is larger because earlier commits already added stuck-recovery reason plumbing, active tool side-effect tracking, and diagnostic recovery coverage. This round adds a narrow active-model-call correction plus two CI repairs; the proof is current-head and tied to the full PR diff.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** availability / session-state / side-effect replay.
- **Risk explanation:** The change touches active embedded-run abort classification. Incorrect classification could affect session cleanup, user aborts, restart aborts, timeout retry/fallback behavior, or side-effecting tool replay safety.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff preserves user/restart/superseded abort behavior, does not relax fallback policy, uses active model-call and active tool side-effect state, keeps force-clear cleanup unchanged, and is covered by current-head runtime proof, targeted regression tests, type checks, and current-head CI failure reproductions.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The source defect was an abort-classification contract violation between diagnostic stuck-session recovery and embedded attempt failover. `recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` knew it was reclaiming a stale internal run (`reason: "stuck_recovery"`), but downstream classification previously could treat recovered stuck runs too broadly as model idle timeouts. That fixed the model-stream stall trace but was unsafe for stale post-model or side-effecting tool states.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** This fixes the owner boundary where replay semantics must be decided. Diagnostic recovery preserves `abortReason: "stuck_recovery"`, but the active attempt checks whether a model call is currently active and whether any active potential-side-effect tool execution exists before choosing abort semantics. The existing failover policy remains the source of truth for whether model idle timeouts rotate/fallback and for keeping tool-execution timeouts out of replay paths.
- **Scope boundary:** The root-cause boundary is attempt-local classification after diagnostic recovery aborts an active embedded run; provider parsing and failover policy are intentionally left unchanged.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth ownership stays in diagnostic recovery, the embedded run registry, attempt-local stuck-recovery classification, diagnostic model-call terminal events, and active tool replay-safety state. The fix classifies before failover observes abort semantics rather than adding downstream string checks or provider-specific special cases.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the downstream recovery-classification boundary; maintainer decision still required on landing order relative to provider-owned opencode-go stream guard PRs and on the unavailable third-party live provider proof.
- **Patch quality notes:** Focused runner-state fix with regression coverage; no provider-specific special case, no parser fallback, no scope expansion, no public contract change, and no broad failover-policy relaxation. The only new catch in `scripts/e2e/lib/openai-chat-tools/client.mjs` is in the tooling test client cleanup path to ignore cancellation errors after a timeout while still awaiting `reader.cancel()` so the response closes before exit.

## Public contract review

- **Contract surface:** Internal embedded-run abort reason plumbing, diagnostic model-call observation lifecycle, attempt-local recovery classification, test mock exports, and tooling test-client timeout cleanup only.
- **Related open PR scan:** #93965 and #93640 cover provider-boundary stream handling. This PR does not alter public API, protocol, config, schema, provider catalog, model defaults, or gateway config.
- **Compatibility / default behavior:** Existing `user_abort`, `restart`, and `superseded` semantics remain external aborts. Existing failover policy remains unchanged. The test-only `agent-scope.js` partial mocks keep unmocked exports on the real module to avoid non-isolated Vitest leakage.
- **Out of scope:** Live provider transport behavior, opencode-go SSE parsing, fallback model selection policy, cron scheduler timeout policy, and broader tool replay-policy changes are intentionally unchanged.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
